title: The people behind the papers - Haley Brown and Justin Kumar.
abstract: Drosophila imaginal discs serve as great models to study chromatin plasticity and regulation. In their work, Justin Kumar and colleagues reveal that distinct Polycomb Group proteins use different molecular mechanisms to repress eye-to-wing transformation in Drosophila under normal development conditions. To find out more about their work, we spoke to the first author, Haley Brown, and the corresponding author, Justin Kumar, Professor of Biology at Indiana University Bloomington, USA.
